items.title: Does Russia's attack on Kharkiv mark a new strategy after Ukraine’s audacious Operation Spider Web?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Several areas in Ukraine, including its second largest city, Kharkiv, were hit by a major Russian bombardment. Ukraine's air force said Russia launched over 200 missiles and drones, almost half of which they were able to down. Ukraine and Russia were set to exchange soldiers’ bodies this weekend. Official Ukrainian sources have said that Ukraine is not delaying the exchange, denying earlier claims by Russia.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Russia launches massive strike on Kharkiv
Konstantin Eggert, DW's Russia Analyst, on Russia's strikes on Kharkiv and advances in Sumy, Ukraine

#russiaukrainewar #putin #sumy

items.title: Could Trump's 'muted' attacks on Musk signal a future makeup – or a plan to prosecute him? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump said he has no plans to talk to billionaire Elon Musk following their public feud. Speaking on board Air Force One, Trump said he was "not particularly interested" in speaking to Musk, dismissing reports of a planned phone call to reconcile things. He threatened again to review government contracts with Musk's businesses, which include Space-X and satellite company Starlink. The extremely influential men fell out, after Musk criticized the president's 'big beautiful bill,' which will see a surge in state spending. On Saturday, Trump signalled that there were would be "consequences" for Musk if he were to donate to the Democratic Party. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Trump threatens to withdraw subsidies from Elon Musk's SpaceX and Tesla contracts
0:38 Tyson Barker, Non-Resident Fellow at the Atlantic Council's Europe Center, on political implications of Trump and Musk's fallout

#elonmusk #trumpmusk #spacex

items.title: Los Angeles riot police fire 'flash-bang' rounds at protesters after violent immigration raids
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration's crackdown on immigration has set off violent protests in Los Angeles. US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents arrested dozens of people in raids across the city, prompting LA mayor Karen Bass to accuse immigration agents of sowing terror in communities. On Friday evening, more than 500 people gathered in downtown Los Angeles to protest ICE’s crackdown. Groups of protesters demonstrated in front of a federal building where immigrants were believed to be detained, demanding their release. There were also reports of protesters vandalizing buildings and throwing slabs of concrete. Los Angeles police, who were not involved in the ICE raids, but were deployed in response to the protests, fired flash grenades at demonstrators. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 ICE agents in unmarked vehicles raid various locations in Los Angeles to make immigration arrests
2:45 Eva Bitran, Director of Immigrant's Rights, ACLU, on unconstitutional and unlawful tactics used by ICE agents
5:44 Jayson Campadonia, NBC News and iHeart Radio, on incidents of vandalism and violence at anti-ICE protests in downtown LA

#iceraids #lapd #iceprotests
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.content:

items.title: Russia launches 'most powerful attack since start of war' on Kharkiv, says city's mayor | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine says Russia launched more than 200 missiles and drones in one of the biggest air attacks in recent weeks. Less than half were intercepted. 
Russian strikes killed at least three people and injured more than 20 in Kharkiv.
Dozens of homes were damaged in the most extensive strike on the city since the start of the war.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Russia launches missiles and drones at Ukraine overnight
0:36 Marina Miron, military analyst, King's College London, on significance of Russia's strike on Ukraine's second largest city

items.title: What's behind the charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>President Trump's crackdown on immigration has sparked protests in Los Angeles, where at least 44 people have been arrested in a series of raids. 
Protesters attempted to stop vehicles from carrying people away. Crowds gathered outside the city's federal detention facility, chanting "set them free, let them stay." The Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, or ICE, says it has carried out an average of 1,600 arrests a day under the Trump administration. 
The clashes came on the same day that the man who became a symbol for opponents of Trump's immigration policy arrived back in the US. Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported from Maryland to El Salvador. Government officials had defied court orders to help bring him back - until Friday. DW's Benjamin Alvarez Gruber reports from Washington DC.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Right-wing Israeli group blocks aid trucks from reaching Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tsav 9 is an Israeli right-wing extremist group attempting to physically block most aid trucks from reaching Gaza through the Kerem Shalom crossing. On Friday, French anti-terror prosecutors announced they have opened a probe into "complicity in genocide" and "incitement to genocide" over French-Israeli members of groups, including Tsav 9, suspected of having blocked aid intended for war-torn Gaza, where according to the UN, many Palestinians are facing starvation.

items.title: Gazans mark Eid al-Adha as malnutrition among children triples | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Muslims across the globe are celebrating Eid al-Adha, one of the most important events on the Islamic calendar. But in Gaza, Palestinian celebrants are marking their second Eid al-Adha under Israeli bombardment. Israel's blockade has left most families with very little to eat during the festival - which is usually marked by sharing meals together.

00:00 Gazans mark Eid Al-Adha amid widespread hunger
03:18 DW speaks with UNICEF spokesman James Elder in Gaza
07:39 GHF opens two new aid sites
09:24 DW speaks with Olga Cherevko of UN OCHA

items.title: Sudan: The world's largest humanitarian crisis no one is talking about | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The ongoing civil war in Sudan has left millions displaced and tens of thousands dead. Half of the population, 25 million people, now face severe hunger in what the UN calls the world's largest food and humanitarian crisis. 
Sudan's community kitchens have become lifelines for survival. They are run by volunteers, but since the US cut aid funding in January, more than half of these kitchens have been forced to close.

00:00 Sudan's volunteer food kitchens forced to close
04:11 DW speaks with Rania Dagash-Kamara, assistant executive director for partnerships and resource mobilization at the World Food Programme

items.title: Chiquita fires thousands of striking banana workers and shuts down operations | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Bananas are one of Panama's five most important exports — and it is precisely in this sector that disputes and redundancies are now occurring. The Panamanian subsidiary of the American banana company Chiquita has announced its intention to make all of its remaining employees in the Central American country redundant.
During protests against pension reforms in May, Chiquita laid off thousands of harvest workers in Panama. A court ruled the strike illegal. With work still at a standstill, the US banana giant now intends to lay off its remaining workers.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
